title: Combined Business and Personal Travel Deduction Optimization

requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must properly allocate combined business and personal travel expenses to maximize deductible business portion while maintaining proper substantiation and meeting primary purpose requirements
criteria: Primary purpose of trip must be business to deduct transportation costs for domestic travel
Business days generally include days conducting business, travel days, weekends/holidays between business days
Personal days require allocation of lodging, meals, and incidental expenses (transportation still deductible if primary purpose business)
Foreign travel requires allocation of transportation costs unless trip entirely business or meets specific exceptions
Foreign travel exception: Outside U.S. 7 days or less, or personal days less than 25% of total days and no substantial control
Document business purpose, activities, contacts, and outcomes for each business day with contemporaneous records
Deduct 50% of business meals (100% if meeting specific requirements under temporary provisions)
Personal travel days expenses (lodging, meals, entertainment) not deductible even on primarily business trip

travel_allocation_rules.domestic_travel: If primary purpose business, entire transportation deductible; allocate lodging and meals by days
travel_allocation_rules.foreign_travel: Must allocate transportation costs unless entire trip business or meets specific time/control exceptions
travel_allocation_rules.primary_purpose_test: Compare number of business days to personal days; business must predominate for transportation deduction
travel_allocation_rules.business_day_definition: Day where principal activity is business pursuit; includes travel days and standby days
travel_allocation_rules.spouse_travel: Spouse travel generally not deductible unless bona fide business purpose and employee of business
travel_allocation_rules.per_diem_option: Can use IRS per diem rates for meals and lodging simplifying substantiation for business days

restrictions: Personal portion of travel expenses never deductible even if business purpose exists for other portions
Convention and seminar travel subject to additional substantiation and reasonableness requirements
Foreign convention deduction limited to conventions in North American area unless specific exception applies
Cruise ship convention deduction limited to $2,000 annually and requires U.S. flag vessel and ports
Investment seminars and conventions not deductible regardless of business purpose or benefit
Lavish or extravagant travel expenses subject to disallowance even if business purpose substantiated
Additional recordkeeping required showing time spent on business vs personal activities each day
State tax treatment may not conform to federal allocation methods requiring state-specific analysis and planning
